title: Pharmaceutical trial investment in Africa among 10 multinational companies
primary_estimand: Africa extraction ratio (Africa trials / global trials) per company
certainty: high (registry data, complete enumeration within ClinicalTrials.gov)
body: Among the ten largest multinational pharmaceutical companies, how much of the global clinical trial portfolio actually includes African sites? We queried ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 on 27 March 2026 for all interventional trials sponsored by each company worldwide and for the subset with African locations, then calculated an Africa extraction ratio for each company portfolio. Across 36,912 global trials, only 1,331 included African sites, giving a weighted mean extraction ratio of 3.6%. Novo Nordisk had the highest ratio at 7.3%, whereas Johnson and Johnson had the lowest at 0.8% with only 16 African trials among roughly 2,000 worldwide. GlaxoSmithKline showed the clearest examples of Africa-focused malaria and rotavirus programmes, while Merck and Gilead were represented mainly by global registration mega-trials. The gap between likely African revenue exposure and direct research investment is consistent with a structural extraction pattern. ClinicalTrials.gov does not capture trials registered only on national or WHO platforms, so some company activity may be missed.
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used in early drafting. The final text was rewritten by the author, who takes full responsibility for the content.

checks.name: primary estimate in sentence 3-4
checks.detail: Sentence 3 reports 3.6% mean extraction ratio; sentence 4 reports extremes.
